evals.name: events-lifecycle-stream
evals.prompt: Write a Ruby script using the AG-UI SDK that starts a run, streams a text message containing 'Hello, World!', and then finishes the run successfully.
evals.expected_output: The script should correctly emit RunStartedEvent, TextMessageStartEvent, TextMessageContentEvent(s), TextMessageEndEvent, and RunFinishedEvent in that order.
expectations: The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Events::RunStartedEvent
The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Events::TextMessageStartEvent, TextMessageContentEvent, and TextMessageEndEvent with matching message_id
The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Events::RunFinishedEvent at the end
The code uses AgUiProtocol::Encoder::EventEncoder to encode events and writes them to a stream

evals.name: types-multimodal-runinput
evals.prompt: Write a Ruby script that creates a multimodal UserMessage with TextInputContent and BinaryInputContent, and constructs a complete RunAgentInput object.
evals.expected_output: The script should initialize UserMessage, TextInputContent, BinaryInputContent, and RunAgentInput correctly using the AG-UI Ruby SDK types.
expectations: The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Types::UserMessage
The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Types::TextInputContent and AgUiProtocol::Core::Types::BinaryInputContent
The code initializes UserMessage with content as an array of TextInputContent and BinaryInputContent
The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Types::RunAgentInput with thread_id, run_id, state, messages, tools, context, forwarded_props

evals.name: rails-streaming-patterns
evals.prompt: Implement a Rails controller using the AG-UI Ruby SDK that supports two streaming patterns: (1) with_stream with rack.hijack for client streaming, (2) ActionController::Live with text/event-stream content type and client disconnect (IOError) handling. Clean up/close the stream in ensure blocks.
evals.expected_output: A Rails controller containing both streaming endpoints, using proper SSE headers, rack.hijack proc, ActionController::Live, IOError rescue, and ensure blocks.
expectations: The controller sets Content-Type, Cache-Control, and X-Accel-Buffering headers
One action implements the with_stream pattern using response.headers['rack.hijack'] and proc
One action includes ActionController::Live and writes to response.stream
Both endpoints handle IOError for client disconnect
Both endpoints close the stream in ensure blocks
The controller uses AgUiProtocol::Encoder::EventEncoder to encode events before writing to the streams
The endpoints stream specific AG-UI events such as RunStartedEvent and RunFinishedEvent

evals.name: references-docs-usage
evals.prompt: Based on the AG-UI Ruby SDK documentation, explain the difference between TextMessageStartEvent and TextMessageChunkEvent, listing the attributes required by each.
evals.expected_output: An explanation showing that TextMessageStartEvent marks the start of a message with message_id, while TextMessageChunkEvent is a convenience chunk containing both the message_id and text delta directly, including their attributes.
expectations: The response explicitly mentions reading reference files (e.g. references/events.md) for SDK details
The response accurately details TextMessageStartEvent attributes (message_id, parent_id, role, index, etc.)
The response accurately details TextMessageChunkEvent attributes (message_id, delta, index)

evals.name: reasoning-events-stream
evals.prompt: Write a Ruby script using the AG-UI SDK that emits a complete reasoning block with streaming reasoning messages. Include ReasoningStartEvent, ReasoningMessageStartEvent, ReasoningMessageContentEvent(s), ReasoningMessageEndEvent, and ReasoningEndEvent.
evals.expected_output: The script should correctly emit all reasoning event types in the proper sequence for a reasoning block.
expectations: The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Events::ReasoningStartEvent with a message_id
The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Events::ReasoningMessageStartEvent with a different message_id and role 'reasoning'
The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Events::ReasoningMessageContentEvent with matching message_id and non-empty delta
The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Events::ReasoningMessageEndEvent to close the reasoning message
The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Events::ReasoningEndEvent to close the reasoning block
The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Events::ReasoningEncryptedValueEvent for encrypted content

evals.name: reasoning-message-type
evals.prompt: Create a ReasoningMessage using the AG-UI Ruby SDK types and explain its properties including the encrypted_value field for zero-data-retention mode.
evals.expected_output: A ReasoningMessage with id, content, and optional encrypted_value property documented.
expectations: The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Types::ReasoningMessage
The ReasoningMessage has role automatically set to 'reasoning'
The code demonstrates encrypted_value for zero-data-retention mode

evals.name: interrupt-resume-types
evals.prompt: Create an Interrupt type and a ResumeEntry type using the AG-UI Ruby SDK for a human-in-the-loop workflow. Show how to resolve an interrupt with a payload.
evals.expected_output: Interrupt and ResumeEntry objects with appropriate properties for HITL workflows.
expectations: The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Types::Interrupt with id, reason, message, tool_call_id, response_schema, expires_at, metadata
The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Types::ResumeEntry with interrupt_id, status, and payload
The code uses RunFinishedInterruptOutcome with an array of Interrupt objects

evals.name: agent-capabilities-declaration
evals.prompt: Create an AgentCapabilities object using the AG-UI Ruby SDK that declares: identity (name, version), transport (streaming, websocket), tools (supported, parallel_calls), state (snapshots, deltas), reasoning (supported, streaming), multimodal (input: image, audio; output: audio), and human_in_the_loop (interrupts).
evals.expected_output: A complete AgentCapabilities object with nested capability objects properly configured.
expectations: The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Capabilities::AgentCapabilities
The code uses IdentityCapabilities with name and version
The code uses TransportCapabilities with streaming and websocket
The code uses ToolsCapabilities with supported and parallel_calls
The code uses StateCapabilities with snapshots and deltas
The code uses ReasoningCapabilities with supported and streaming
The code uses MultimodalCapabilities with MultimodalInputCapabilities and MultimodalOutputCapabilities
The code uses HumanInTheLoopCapabilities with interrupts

evals.name: multimodal-content-types
evals.prompt: Create multimodal content types (ImageInputContent, AudioInputContent, VideoInputContent, DocumentInputContent) using the AG-UI Ruby SDK with URL sources.
evals.expected_output: All four multimodal content types properly initialized with source and metadata.
expectations: The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Types::ImageInputContent with InputContentUrlSource
The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Types::AudioInputContent with InputContentUrlSource
The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Types::VideoInputContent with InputContentUrlSource
The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Types::DocumentInputContent with InputContentUrlSource
The code uses AgUiProtocol::Core::Types::InputContentDataSource for base64 encoded content
